arts.ttl: Southampton v Tottenham: FPL team news + predicted line-ups
arts.sum: Team form, absentees and our predicted XIs The post Southampton v Tottenham: FPL team news + predicted line-ups appeared first on Best FPL Tips, Advice, Team News, Picks, and Statistics from Fantasy Football Scout.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou fights on as Tottenham return to scene of Antonio Conte rant
arts.sum: A 3-3 draw at Southampton in March 2023 proved to be Conte’s final match in charge of Spurs after a post-match tirade against his players and board.

arts.ttl: Big Postecoglou U-turn will either sink or steer his Tottenham tenure after awkward Son moment
arts.sum: I’m asking 18-year-olds to do some massive jobs and you want senior players, and he’s a senior player, he's an international and he's won the Champions League, there’s a level of application and performance you need to rise to, to help the team," he said. Porro and Udogie have played so many minutes in recent weeks that surely Djed Spence at least needs to get his first start for Tottenham on Sunday at St Mary's and you know things are bad in terms of squad depth when Sergio Reguilon could also come into contention, a player who was meant to have left in the summer.

arts.ttl: Postecoglou to 'double down' on Spurs approach
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou says he loves "being in the middle of a storm" and intends to "double down" on his principles amid his team's poor run of form.

arts.ttl: Tottenham 'launch' another review of medical department amid injury crisis
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur have reportedly launched a second review in less than 12 months into their medical department due to the mounting injuries in the squad. Ben Davies has a thigh injury, while both defenders Cristian Romero and Micky van de Ven returned from their own injury lay-offs over the weekend, but suffered new injuries during the defeat to Chelsea .

arts.ttl: Report: £12m midfielder likened to Saul Niguez has caught Ange Postecoglou's eye ahead of transfer window
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur are one of the Premier League clubs that could benefit the most from the arrival of the January transfer window. Spurs have endured...

arts.ttl: Man Utd receive SHOCK Tyler Dibling update
arts.sum: However, while Dibling is yet to sign a contract extension at Southampton, Saints boss Russell Martin has revealed that the 18-year-old’s left foot would cost at least €25m. Southampton boss Russell Martin has suggested that it will cost Manchester United in excess of €50 million to sign Tyler Dibling next summer.

arts.ttl: Tottenham’s Ange Postecoglou will not spare ‘bruised egos’ amid injury crisis
arts.sum: Manager stands by ‘assessment’ of Timo Werner display Mikel Arteta relaxed about finding new Arsenal director Ange Postecoglou has stood by his “assessment” of Timo Werner’s performance in Tottenham’s 1-1 Europa League draw at Rangers, and insisted he has no time for “bruised egos” with his team in turmoil. Injury-hit Spurs were able to fight back through Dejan Kulusevski’s 75th-minute leveller to leave Ibrox with a point, but Postecoglou insisted after Thursday’s game that the display of Werner, who was replaced at half-time, “wasn’t acceptable”. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Tottenham January transfer priority now perfectly clear after unexpected £6m twist - Kieran Horn
arts.sum: With the Italy international set to miss at least two months, talk of Tottenham signing a new goalkeeper in January arose but to Postecoglou that was never an obvious option with Fraser Forster more than ready to fill in. While many wanted a new shot-stopper signed in January, with it likely that is something done regardless very soon, Forster has made it clear he can fill Vicario's boots for a bit longer and if anything showed the position that needs recruitment.

arts.ttl: Southampton boss Russell Martin admits he's been left 'incredibly hurt and embarrassed'
arts.sum: Southampton are bottom of the Premier League table with only one win to their name so far this term and only five points in total, having lost 12 of their 15 games thus far this term
